subject: english
learning_objectives: Define conjunctions and identify the three types with examples
Define prepositions and demonstrate their use with collocations
Use conjunctions and prepositions correctly in sentences

sections.heading: Conjunctions – Definition
sections.content: A conjunction is a word that joins words or groups of words together.

sections.heading: Types of Conjunctions
sections.content:
Co-ordinating Conjunctions – join words or groups of equal grammatical rank.
  and, or, but
  Joy and Jane | Bolu or Joy | We came but you were not around.

Correlative Conjunctions – used in pairs.
  either…or | not only…but also | both…and | neither…nor
  Both James and Jerry attended the party.
  She is not only intelligent but also kind.
  Either the teacher or the man comes here regularly.

Subordinating Conjunctions – introduce subordinate clauses.
  after, because, before, if, in order that, since, when, who, whose, that, which
  He left when she was cooking. | We cooked before they arrived.

sections.heading: Prepositions – Definition
sections.content: A preposition shows the relationship between two words in a sentence. Examples: within, before, at, in, on, over.

sections.heading: Preposition Collocations
sections.content:
allergic to | subjected to | arrive in | live in/on/at | stare at | indict for | convicted of | victim of | down with (fever) | charge with | abide by (rule) | good at | bad at | frown at | consist of | confidence in | confide in | senior to | junior to | related to | agree to (a plan) | agree with (a person) | popular with | comply with | blame on | hinges on | wait upon

Complex prepositions:
in agreement with | in compliance with | because of | in accordance with | for the sake of | on account of | with regard to | with reference to | in spite of | by reason of | in regard to | by means of | along with | contrary to | in addition to
